steps.title: Separate decrease from sameness
steps.content: The sentence already excludes countries with increases and asks for examples of the other two patterns: a lower number in 2013 than in 2009 and the same number in both years. The graph shows the Philippines decreasing, Belgium staying the same, and Guatemala increasing.

steps.title: Reject the wrong patterns
steps.content: Choice B shifts to a 2013 cross-country comparison instead of change over time. Choice C uses Guatemala, which increased, so it does not provide a same-number example. Choice D also uses Guatemala, so it repeats the already excluded increase pattern. The answer is A.
